findings.finding: DIRECTIVE-20260509-001 completed: PROP-025 authored covering content-based curmudgeon batch gate.
findings.design_decision: Selected Option B (decider sets explicit class field) with absent-field default = 'deep-attack' for backward-compat. Rejected Option A (regex over reason text — brittle), Option C (patch-size + cycle heuristic — too many false positives), and Option D (hybrid A+B — unnecessary regex code path; legacy items drain in <24h under safe default).
findings.byte_budget_calibration: Recommended 20KB combined cap for verification class (was 10KB), 10KB unchanged for deep-attack class. Conservative single-step relaxation; directive suggested up to 30KB but proposed 20KB and revisit after 30 days of empirical data.
findings.carve_out_retired: The 'EXP integration NOT batchable, regardless of severity' clause in current gate (3) is the bigger practical blocker than the gate (1) severity check — the four observed 2026-05-09 singletons would have failed the carve-out before gate (1) ran. PROP-025 retires the carve-out in favor of class-based discrimination.
open_questions_for_operator: Q1: Is 'EXP integration → class=verification' correct in all cases, or should EXPs that introduce NEW substantive arguments be class=deep-attack? (PROP-025 recommends adding an EXP-side `introduces_new_argument: bool` field for analyst to set.)
Q2: 20KB or 30KB for verification-class byte cap? (PROP-025 proposes 20KB conservatively.)
Q3: Bump priority-queue.json schema_version=2 at land-time, or defer? (PROP-025 recommends bumping with audit fields.)
Q4: Update CLAUDE.md operator-runbook to mention class field for operator pushes? (PROP-025 recommends a one-sentence addition.)

findings.finding: FUSE workspace md5 mismatch on docs/index.html.
findings.evidence: MD5 check during Mode 2 quick-check: workspace docs/index.html != raw.githubusercontent main copy. data/wins.json, data/sections.json, build-scripts/digest-reviews.js all match.
findings.diagnosis: Likely a pending workspace-sync cycle gap — workspace-sync runs hourly and may not have ferried the last build's HTML output yet. Self-resolves within 1h. Not blocking any agent (decider rebuilds in its own clone; build pipeline doesn't read docs/index.html).
findings.recommendation: No action. Re-check on next tinker run; if still stale after 4h, investigate workspace-sync logs.

findings.evidence: du -sm reports 308MB at /tmp/tinker-inspect (a 50-commit shallow clone created this run for git-history inspection of the 2026-05-09 priority-queue pushes). Ambient FUSE+other footprint ~370MB.
findings.recommendation: Self-cleared at run end. /tmp/tinker-inspect will be `rm -rf`'d in the cleanup step. Future tinker runs that need git history could read directly from the operator's fresh clone if one exists at /tmp/edit-clone, avoiding the 308MB temporary.
